seoTitle: How to Install Zephyr in Jira
metaDescription: Install the Zephyr test management app in Jira so your team can plan, execute, and track test cases alongside your issues.
intro: Testing teams often need more structured test case management than Jira offers natively, and Zephyr fills that gap directly inside your existing projects. This guide covers installing Zephyr and getting it connected to Jira.

steps.heading: Go to the Atlassian Marketplace
steps.body: From your Jira admin settings, open the Apps section and search the Marketplace for Zephyr.

steps.heading: Choose the right Zephyr version
steps.body: Select the Zephyr product that matches your needs, since there are different versions built for different testing workflows.

steps.heading: Start the free trial or purchase
steps.body: Install the app, which usually starts with a free trial period before requiring a paid subscription.

steps.heading: Configure Zephyr in your project
steps.body: Add the Zephyr features to the relevant project so test cases and cycles become available there.

steps.heading: Create your first test case
steps.body: Open the new Zephyr panel within a project and create a test case to confirm the installation is working.

faq.question: Is Zephyr free to use in Jira?
faq.answer: Zephyr typically offers a free trial, but ongoing use requires a paid subscription through the Atlassian Marketplace.

faq.question: Do I need admin access to install Zephyr?
faq.answer: Yes, installing apps from the Marketplace requires Jira administrator permissions.

faq.question: Can Zephyr test cases link to existing Jira issues?
faq.answer: Yes, Zephyr test cases can be linked to stories or bugs so testing traces back to the related development work.

faq.question: Which Zephyr version should I choose?
faq.answer: This depends on your testing needs; simpler teams often start with the more lightweight version before considering enterprise options.
